_about: C29 model-floor stylesheet (Software Factory v4, sweep-1). A CSS-like, cost-aware routing policy that maps roles/task-classes to a chosen model, declares a minimum-acceptable model (the FLOOR) per role/task, tags each model with a family, and carries the cross-family policy flag. See README.md and spec C29-model-floor-stylesheet.md.

policy._cross_family_required_note: Advisory default at Phase-0 (per spec D-1/FE-1): a same-provider judge isolated by rig/role/prompt is the sanctioned baseline. Flipping this to true is a Gate-B4 trust lever: it would require the judge to run on a DIFFERENT provider family than the coder (cross-provider holdout). When false, route.py only EMITS an advisory note; it never refuses a route on family grounds. When true, a judge whose family == coder family is a hard violation (non-zero exit).
policy._independence_level_note: L0=none, L1=same-provider judge isolated by rig/role/prompt (Phase-0 default), L2/L3=cross-family/cross-provider (FE-1).

registry._about: modeldb-shaped: {id, family, cost_tier, rank}. 'rank' is the capability ordering used by the floor comparison (higher = more capable). 'family' is what the cross-family rule compares; all current models share family 'anthropic' (a non-Anthropic provider would be a different family, i.e. cross-family).

rules._about: Maps role + task_class to a chosen model and a declared floor (minimum acceptable model). 'floor' is enforced: a requested_model below the floor rank is upgraded to the floor (or refused). Lookup precedence: exact (role,task_class) -> role default ('*' task_class) -> global default.
